key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network can infer laser welding penetration state, depth, and weld seam morphology directly from weld pool images and process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ quality monitoring and closed-loop control for laser penetration welding, reducing reliance on destructive post-process inspection.
method: Uses top-view weld pool images from a CMOS camera as the main visual input.
Fuses spatial-temporal features with welding parameters in a CNN plus state-space-model framework.
Applies a dataset construction strategy designed to improve robustness and generalization.
evidence_signal: On the test set, penetration-state accuracy reached 99.35%, penetration-depth error was 1.79 mm, and weld cross-section reconstruction accuracy was 95.65%.
watch_item: Performance may depend on camera setup, weld geometry, and the representativeness of the training dataset, so transfer to new welding conditions should be validated carefully.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
